title: Slug auto-recycles on close; new issue can shadow closed one for discovery
body:
# Symptom

Slug uniqueness is enforced across OPEN issues only. Closing an
issue silently frees its slug, so the next `jjf new --slug
<freed-slug>` succeeds and shadows the closed one for
discovery.

# Repro

```
$ jjf new -t Roadmap1 --type roadmap --slug roadmap
$ jjf new -t Roadmap2 --type roadmap --slug roadmap
{"ok":false,"error":{"kind":"slug_collision", ...}}
$ jjf close <roadmap1>
$ jjf new -t Roadmap3 --type roadmap --slug roadmap
{"ok":true, ...}    # the slug 'roadmap' now resolves to roadmap3
$ jjf show roadmap
# resolves to the new one; the old roadmap1 is harder to find
```

# Design call

Two reasonable policies:

1. **Slug uniqueness across all statuses** (open + closed +
   blocked). Closed issues retain their slug forever; new
   tickets must pick a new one. Trades a small naming friction
   for unambiguous discovery.
2. **Warn-but-allow on re-use** (current behavior + warning).
   `jjf new --slug roadmap` succeeds but prints `(slug
   previously held by closed issue f2a71ff)` on stderr.

Either is defensible; the current silent re-use is the wrong
default for an audit-trail-flavored planner.

# Confidence

Reproducible 100%. Tested in `scratchpad/qa/r08-roadmaps/`.
